task_completion.description: Create and activate a new vehicle profile named 'Supply Vehicle' with specific parameters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the Sygic vehicle profiles list or edit screen showing 'Supply Vehicle' selected as active, with parameters: Fuel type Diesel, Year 2020, Emission Euro6, and Max speed 80 km/h.

task_completion.description: Set route calculation preference to Shortest.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the Sygic Route Computing settings menu showing 'Shortest' selected instead of 'Fastest'.

task_completion.description: Configure road avoidances to allow unpaved roads and avoid ferries.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the Sygic routing/avoidance settings showing 'Unpaved roads' toggled OFF (allowed) and 'Ferries' toggled ON (avoided).
Screenshot of the Sygic routing settings showing 'Arrive in driving direction' toggled ON.

task_completion.description: Plan a route from Kabul to Kandahar with an intermediate stop in Jalalabad.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the route overview or itinerary screen showing the correct sequence of waypoints: Kabul (Start) -> Jalalabad (Waypoint) -> Kandahar (Destination).

task_completion.description: Add a gas station waypoint using the 'On Route' filter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot showing a gas station added to the active route itinerary, or the 'Places on Route' search screen actively being used to select a gas station.

integrity.description: The agent used the Sygic GUI to configure settings and plan the route, not terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Sygic app interface being interacted with. There should be no terminal windows executing `sqlite3` or `sed` commands to modify the app's databases or XML files.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a text editor to manually change preference files.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like nano, vim, or Android file editors) are visible editing `com.sygic.aura_preferences.xml` or `base_persistence_preferences.xml`.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely searched for the cities and calculated the route in the app.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of intermediate search screens for Kabul, Jalalabad, and Kandahar, and the map rendering the route line between these cities.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must use the Sygic GPS app. The injected vehicle profile 'Personal Sedan' must be replaced or ignored, and a new profile 'Supply Vehicle' must be created and set as active with parameters: DIESEL fuel, 2020 production year, EURO6 emission standard, and 80 km/h max speed. In the app's routing settings, 'Route computing' must be set to 'Shortest'. In the avoidance settings, 'Unpaved roads' must be allowed (toggled off/false) and 'Ferries' must be avoided (toggled on/true). 'Arrive in driving direction' must be enabled. The route must explicitly show Kabul as start, Jalalabad as a waypoint, and Kandahar as the destination. A gas station must be added to this route using the 'Places on Route' / 'On Route' POI search feature.
